problem: src/output/builder/single/module_details.rs::build_seo_details hardcoded German labels (meta_tags, SchemaExtracted match arms, signal_rows rating bands, identity_facts, page_profile_facts, heading_summary, social_summary, technical_summary, tracking_summary — roughly 50 strings) regardless of the report's configured locale, so an EN-locale report still showed German SEO section text.
evidence: Found via a full localization sweep of build_seo_details after an initial 4-string spot fix (Seitentitel/Inhaltstyp/Themenhinweise/Seitentyp) turned out to be far from complete; confirmed missing en-branch coverage for meta tag labels, schema-extracted counts, rating bands, and ~15 tracking_summary entries (Google Fonts, Tracking cookies, Cookie inventory, Browser Storage, Consent cookies, Zaraz, etc).
expected: Every user-visible string build_seo_details emits has both a de and en branch selected by the report's locale, per this project's localization architecture (JSON canonical English, PDF derives locale at render time).
regression: src/output/pdf/tests.rs::test_seo_details_english_locale_has_no_known_german_leaks (asserts EN labels present AND specific German strings absent in EN-locale Typst source).
counter_examples: The German-locale rendering of the same report must still show the original German labels — this is a locale-selection fix, not a removal of German text.
